title: Children's Out of Home Respite Care * At Risk Families - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Children's Out of Home Respite Care * At Risk Families 14 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter At Risk Families Disabilities Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers comprehensive family support for families with children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Services include therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, family support and individualized case management, home visiting, parenting education, and group activities designed to strengthen parenting skills and promote healthy child development 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Young children ages 0-5 who are experiencing high levels of stress or who have experienced trauma and/or are at risk for abuse and neglect; parents who have multiple risk factors and are struggling to nurture and care for their children. High-risk families typically experience several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single parenthood, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, history with drugs and alcohol, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 720 N 14th Street Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY NORTH DOUGLAS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 239 East B Avenue Drain, OR 97435 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY SOUTH LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 75 S 4th street Creswell, OR 97426 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW GLADSTONE CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nurseries serve families with young children who are most at risk of abuse and neglect. They provide high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. LifeWorks NW provides wraparound services to high-risk families. Services include early childhood intervention and social, emotional, and family support. Also provides mental health services and trauma assessments, traumatic stress expertise, home visitations, parent/infant and parent/toddler classes, art and music therapy, respite child care, and weekly parenting classes using the "Making Parenting a Pleasure" curriculum. Parents/children must be enrolled to receive most services. 18907 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Caregivers with children ages 0-5 and women in their 3rd trimester of pregnancy Serves high-risk families. "High-risk" typically means experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent households, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: By appointment Email (541) 907-1987 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PIONEER RELIEF NURSERY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides family support, referrals, advocacy, respite care, and parenting classes to parents who are experiencing high levels of stress. Therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers and home visits provided as well. 1312 SW 2nd Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Parents experiencing stress with children ages 0-6 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 215-1017 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TREASURE VALLEY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves families with children who are at risk of abuse and neglect by providing intensive support through individualized case management. Also provides parenting education, home visits, developmental assessments, group activities, and referrals to other community resources. Mental health care, counseling for domestic violence, and support for adults recovering from addiction are offered as well. 780 SE 6th Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: -High-risk families with children ages 0-5. Families with children younger than 18 months are served through home visits only -High-risk typically means families experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent household, criminal history, adult survivor of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Classroom sessions: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday. No classes most major holidays including Christmas break. Email (541) 823-2526 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY EAST PORTLAND FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting education, support through home visits, and group activities and intensive case management. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 2609 SE 136th Avenue Portland, OR 97236 Eligibility: High risk families with children ages 6 weeks to 5 years old. Intakes are completed to determine eligibility.
